Blues guitar evolved from the rich traditions of work songs, field hollers, spirituals, and ragtime. One of the earliest and most influential figures in developing the distinctive sound of the blues guitar was Robert Johnson, who emerged in the 1930s. Kennedy's assassination on November 22, 1963, Governor John B. Connally of Texas was also seriously injured. Governor Connally sustained multiple wounds, including a shattered rib, a broken shoulder blade, a damaged lung, and a fractured sternum. His injuries were severe but not fatal, and he played a significant role in the recovery efforts and investigations that followed the tragic event.
